description_long: This helicopter features a classic utility design with a streamlined fuselage painted in a dark green and gray camouflage pattern, accented by bright orange panels around the cabin doors and windows. The main rotor is long and slender, with four blades extending from a central hub, while the tail boom is elongated and supports a smaller tail rotor. The cockpit is equipped with large, rectangular windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing excellent visibility for pilots. The landing gear consists of two robust, black skids connected by crossbars, designed for stable ground contact. Markings, including a registration number and insignia, are visible on the tail boom and fuselage. The overall build suggests a military or utility helicopter suitable for urban operations, search and rescue, or transport.
description: A military-style helicopter with a green and gray camouflage body, orange cabin panels, large cockpit windows, and black landing skids. It features a four-blade main rotor and visible registration markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, orange cabin panels, large cockpit windows, and the main and tail rotors.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the cockpit windows, nose, and the symmetrical extension of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail boom, orange cabin panels, and the registration markings on the fuselage.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades made of composite or metal, colored gray with no visible markings, each blade extending nearly the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: A two-blade tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored gray with a small protective fin.
attribute_landing_skids: Two black, tubular landing skids with crossbars for stability, made of metal, extending the length of the fuselage and slightly curved at the ends.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Five large, rectangular cockpit windows wrapping around the front and sides, clear with black framing, providing wide visibility.
